title: Concurrent canonical saves on same spec folder race description.json memorySequence/lastUpdated
evidence: loadPFD/savePFD pair is non-atomic (compared to hook-state.ts:269-280 tmp+rename); retry-loop mitigates up to 3 attempts but not guaranteed; parallel workflow.ts invocations from two worktrees can produce interleaved lastUpdated timestamps

rationale: POSIX 0o600 on hook-state files + 0o700 on state dir (hook-state.ts:180,278) blocks cross-UID read; stdio transport (context-server.ts:425) not accessible to different-UID attacker
webStandardStreamableHttp.js:419 server-generates sessionId; :580-585 validates mcp-session-id header against stored value; context-server.ts:425 hardcodes stdio transport so HTTP surface not mounted
git log 4d3af5a8c..HEAD -- package.json returns zero hits; no postinstall/preinstall scripts in mcp_server/package.json
buildCallerContext context-server.ts:422 spreads extra into fresh {} literal; no Object.assign onto prototype-reachable target; JSON.parse in handlers/save limited to dedup.ts:121 pre-017
caller-context.ts:20 AsyncLocalStorage is per-async-chain isolated by Node runtime; each JSON-RPC request enters own storage.run scope
retry-budget.ts:40-65 has no awaits; Map.get+Map.set atomic per microtask on single-threaded event loop
getProjectHash 12-char prefix = 17-byte filenames under eCryptfs 143-byte cap; Windows advisory POSIX mode is pre-017 baseline, not a Phase 017 regression
P0 severity defensible for single-user workstation deployment (typical); drops to P1 for multi-tenant cross-UID host (uncommon); iter 5 verdict accurate for stated threat model

notes: 0 P0 escalations beyond iter 5 C1 (which is same-UID-scoped P0, not escalated in iter 8)

rationale: convergence conditions met: 2 consecutive iterations below 0.08 threshold (iter 6 0.057, iter 8 0.028); no new P0 surfaces; iter 9/10 best spent actually running vitest suites against HEAD or closing the loop
